verses.text: Put them in mind to obey rulers, 'to speak evil of no man,' to be gentle to all. Good citizens. The Christian is peaceable and kind.
Put them in mind to obey rulers, 'to speak evil of no man,' to be gentle to all. Good citizens. The Christian is peaceable and kind.
We ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, 'serving divers lusts and pleasures.' Remember. Grace found us in our sin.
'Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration.' Saved by mercy. New birth, not our works, saves us.
'Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration.' Saved by mercy. New birth, not our works, saves us.
He shed His Spirit on us abundantly, 'that being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs.' Heirs of life. Grace makes us heirs of eternal life.
He shed His Spirit on us abundantly, 'that being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs.' Heirs of life. Grace makes us heirs of eternal life.
Affirm that believers 'maintain good works'; avoid foolish questions and strivings about the law. Profitable. Faith is fruitful; vain disputes are not.
Affirm that believers 'maintain good works'; avoid foolish questions and strivings about the law. Profitable. Faith is fruitful; vain disputes are not.
A heretick, 'after the first and second admonition, reject.' Limit. The divisive, unrepentant, is to be put away.
A heretick, 'after the first and second admonition, reject.' Limit. The divisive, unrepentant, is to be put away.
Bring the brethren on their journey; 'let ours also learn to maintain good works.' Grace be with you. Closing. He ends with care and blessing.
Bring the brethren on their journey; 'let ours also learn to maintain good works.' Grace be with you. Closing. He ends with care and blessing.
